signature: Unrecognized command line argument 'publish'.*Missing required argument '<Action>'
cause: dotnet tool install -g microsoft.sqlpackage installs a version that uses legacy /Action flag syntax, not the newer 'publish' subcommand style.
resolution: Change 'sqlpackage publish \' to 'sqlpackage /Action:Publish \'
Pass parameters as /SourceFile, /TargetServerName, /TargetDatabaseName, /TargetUser, /TargetPassword
Re-run the workflow

signature: DenyPublicEndpointEnabled|The operation could not complete.*public network access.*disabled
cause: Azure SQL Server has publicNetworkAccess=Disabled. Firewall rules cannot be added while public network access is globally disabled on the server — even with correct RBAC.
resolution: Run 'az sql server update --name <server> --resource-group <rg> --set publicNetworkAccess=Enabled' BEFORE adding the firewall rule
Then add the firewall rule as normal
In cleanup (if: always()), delete the firewall rule AND restore publicNetworkAccess=Disabled
antiPattern: az sql server firewall-rule create (without first enabling publicNetworkAccess)
correctPattern: az sql server update --set publicNetworkAccess=Enabled && az sql server firewall-rule create

cause: Job-level if: conditions are evaluated BEFORE the environment: block loads. Environment-scoped variables and secrets are unavailable in job guards — they evaluate to empty string, making the guard always false.
resolution: Set the variable at REPOSITORY level (Settings > Secrets and variables > Actions > Variables), not only at the environment level
Environment-scoped value can still override the repo-level value during execution
Job if: guard reads the repo-level value; steps inside the job use the environment-level value
antiPattern: if: ${{ vars.MY_VAR != '' }} with MY_VAR set only in the environment (not repo)
correctPattern: Set MY_VAR at repo level for guard visibility; optionally also set at env level for env-specific override

signature: 503 Service Unavailable|Application Error.*starting the application|EADDRINUSE|Cannot find module.*node_modules
cause: App Service 503s most commonly result from: missing env vars (silent startup crash), missing node_modules in deployed artifact, wrong PORT binding (app must use process.env.PORT), or a transient OOM/timeout.
resolution: Stream live logs: 'az webapp log tail --name <app> --resource-group <rg>'
Check App Settings for missing required vars: 'az webapp config appsettings list'
Restart for config/transient issues: 'az webapp restart'
Redeploy for missing dependency issues (restart alone will not fix)
Verify health endpoint returns 200 after recovery
antiPattern: Restarting the app when the issue is a missing dependency (Cannot find module)
correctPattern: Triage via log tail first, then decide restart vs. redeploy based on error signal

signature: remote: Permission to.*denied|The requested URL returned error: 403
cause: In fork+upstream setups, 'origin' points to the personal fork (read-only for the org) and 'upstream' points to the org repo. Pushing to 'origin' returns 403 when the token doesn't have write access to the fork owner's account.
resolution: Run 'git remote -v' to identify which remote is origin vs upstream
Push to 'upstream': 'git push upstream <branch>'
Create the PR against the upstream repo: 'gh pr create --repo ORG/REPO'
Never assume 'origin' is the push target in enterprise fork workflows
antiPattern: git push origin <branch> when origin=personal-fork and target is org repo
correctPattern: git push upstream <branch> && gh pr create --repo ORG/REPO

signature: Unrecognized command line argument 'publish'.*Missing required argument '<Action>'
cause: dotnet tool install -g microsoft.sqlpackage installs a version that uses legacy /Action flag syntax, not the newer 'publish' subcommand style.
resolution: Change 'sqlpackage publish \' to 'sqlpackage /Action:Publish \'
Pass parameters as /SourceFile, /TargetServerName, /TargetDatabaseName, /TargetUser, /TargetPassword
Re-run the workflow
